role_summary: Co-founder of the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee; lead organizer of the Nashville sit-in movement (1960) and the continuation of the Freedom Rides into Mississippi (May 1961) after the original Riders were beaten in Alabama.
ai_reading: Five corpus voices discuss Nash: Joan Trumpauer Mulholland (entry 60, Freedom Rider), Lonnie C. King (entry 80, Atlanta sit-in organizer), Dion Diamond (entry 27, Howard NAG and Freedom Rider), C. T. Vivian (entry 134, SCLC), and James Oscar Jones (entry 57). The cluster is the 1960-61 sit-in-and-Freedom-Rides cohort; the embedding aligns the in-corpus discussion of Nash with exactly the network she organized.
biographical_paragraph: Diane Nash is discussed in this corpus by 5 interviewees across the catalog (Joan Trumpauer Mulholland (entry 60), Lonnie C. King (entry 80), Dion Diamond (entry 27), C. T. Vivian (entry 134), James Oscar Jones (entry 57)), but was not interviewed for the Civil Rights History Project; this catalog page treats Diane Nash as an external figure whose presence in the archive is mediated through other voices. The canonical scholarly source the catalog cites for biographical orientation appears as [src: 1] below.
